Two kinds of searches, one production unit

People land on this page for one of two very different reasons. Some are searching locally — "CNC cutting Kochi," "laser cutting near me," "vinyl printing Kerala," "print shop Kochi" — because they need a job done: a shop sign, a batch of acrylic cut-outs, an engraved nameplate, a vehicle graphic, a short print run. They want it done nearby, quickly, without shipping anything across the state.

Others are searching from outside Kerala entirely — an event, creative or ad agency in Bangalore, Mumbai, Chennai or Delhi that needs a stall production partner, a creative production unit, or an event production team physically based in Kochi, because that's where their client's activation, exhibition stall or campaign is actually happening.

Both groups end up needing the same thing: a facility that owns its own CNC router, laser cutter, vinyl equipment and large-format printers, rather than a broker who subcontracts the work out. This page covers both — what we actually produce, and how each kind of client works with us.

4Core Processes — CNC, Laser, Vinyl, Print
1In-House Kochi Facility
2Kinds of Clients — Local & Pan-India

CNC cutting, laser cutting, vinyl and print — what each actually does

These four processes are the base of everything produced at the facility, whether the job is a single local order or a full stall build for an out-of-state agency.

CNC routing & cutting

CNC routing turns a design file into a precisely cut physical part — 3D lettering, signage components, activation props, or custom cut-outs in wood, acrylic, MDF or metal. It removes the tolerance problems that come with hand-cutting, which matters whether you're ordering one nameplate or fifty identical stall components that all need to fit together the same way.

Laser cutting & engraving

Laser cutting handles fine-detail work that CNC routing isn't built for — intricate logos, plaques, engraved detailing on acrylic, wood or metal. It's the process behind most small, precise local orders, and also behind the fine detailing on larger stall and exhibition builds.

Vinyl printing & cutting

Vinyl covers decals, wall graphics, window branding, vehicle graphics and cut lettering. There's a real difference between print-and-cut vinyl and cut-only vinyl, and application matters as much as the material — a properly prepped, bubble-free install is what separates vinyl branding that looks intentional from vinyl branding that looks like a sticker.

Large-format & digital print

HP Latex and UV-flatbed printing cover banners, backdrops, posters and rigid-panel printing. Material choice — flex, vinyl, fabric, mesh or rigid board — changes durability outdoors versus indoors, and Kerala's monsoon climate is a real factor in that choice, not a footnote: unlaminated print work degrades faster here than in a drier state.

The same CNC router, laser cutter and print equipment produce a single local nameplate and a full stall build for a Bangalore agency — it's one facility, not two.

Stall, creative & event production for agencies outside Kerala

Stall production — exhibition stalls, activation structures, backdrops, standees, temporary kiosks — is a different discipline from a one-off local cutting job. It's engineered for one-time assembly and breakdown, has to account for weight and portability, and is tied to a fixed event date that cannot move. Creative production and event production sit around this same core: turning a campaign concept into the physical pieces that get installed on-site in Kerala.

For an agency not physically in Kochi, the engagement is straightforward: a brief and reference material come in over call, WhatsApp or email; we scope a quote and timeline against the actual event date; specs and materials get signed off remotely; photo and video updates go out through fabrication so nothing is a surprise; and delivery is either a coordinated on-site install across Kerala, or a careful pack-and-dispatch pan-India for your own team to set up.

A few things are worth confirming with any Kochi partner before committing a stall or event build to them: which of the CNC, laser, vinyl and print processes they actually own in-house versus subcontract; who has a single point of contact across the whole job; a turnaround estimate tied to your real deadline, not a generic one; and clarity, upfront, on who is responsible for installation.

Quick take

If a vendor can't say clearly which of CNC, laser cutting, vinyl and print they do themselves versus pass along to someone else, build a bigger timeline buffer — or keep looking.

Trusted By

CNC, laser, vinyl & print for local businesses in Kochi & Kerala

Not every job that comes through the facility is a large agency build. A significant share is local — shops, businesses and individuals across Kochi and Kerala who need a CNC-cut sign, a laser-engraved plaque, a vinyl-cut decal, or a short print run, and want it done nearby rather than shipped in from another state.

For local orders, the process is simpler: bring or send a file, a reference photo, or even just a rough idea, and it gets scoped directly against the machinery on the floor — the same CNC router, laser cutter, vinyl cutter and print equipment used for larger stall and event builds. There's no minimum order size that locks out a single nameplate or a short print run, and turnaround for most local jobs is measured in days, not weeks, since nothing has to travel across a state border first.

Written by the Creatari Studio team — an in-house CNC, laser cutting, vinyl and print production unit based in Kochi, Kerala, serving local businesses across the state and agencies producing stalls, activations and campaigns from outside it. Learn more about Creatari →

Frequently asked questions

Do you take small, one-off CNC or laser cutting jobs, or only large orders?

Both. A lot of what comes through our Kochi facility is one-off or small-batch — a single nameplate, a short run of acrylic cut-outs, a one-time engraving job — alongside larger stall and activation builds. There's no minimum that shuts out a small local order.

I'm in Kochi or elsewhere in Kerala — can I just walk in with a design?

Yes. Local customers can bring a file, a reference photo or even just a rough idea directly to the facility, get it scoped against the CNC router, laser cutter, vinyl cutter or print equipment on the floor, and usually get a same-week turnaround depending on the job.

Do you work directly with agencies based outside Kerala for stall or event production?

Yes — most of the stall, activation and event production work at our Kochi facility is briefed by agencies and brand teams who are not physically in Kerala. Coordination happens over call, WhatsApp and email, with photo and video updates through production.

What's a realistic turnaround for a stall or activation build?

It depends on scale and materials, but most stall and activation builds are scoped against your event date individually rather than quoted as a generic timeline. Share your deadline upfront and we'll confirm what's realistic before you commit.

Can you install a stall or activation on-site, or do you only fabricate and ship?

Both. We install stalls, activation structures and signage directly across Kerala when required, and we pack and dispatch pan-India when an agency's own on-ground team is handling setup.

What exactly can you cut, print or engrave in-house?

CNC routing, laser cutting and engraving, vinyl printing and cutting, and HP Latex and UV-flatbed large-format printing — across wood, acrylic, MDF, metal, PVC and vinyl, all on machines we own and operate ourselves in Kochi.

Need CNC, Laser, Vinyl, Print or Stall Production in Kochi?

Local job or pan-India agency brief — send the details and we'll scope it against what's actually feasible.
